footer.tagline: We turn complex CAPEX projects into clear, globally quotable work packages.

hero.sub: NovaNexus turns complex production projects into clear, structured work packages that can actually be quoted. The platform sits as a neutral layer between buyers and vetted factories, translating drawings, capacity and risk into a shared map both sides can work from.
hero.noteBuyers: For buyers as well as plant / project teams
hero.noteSuppliers: For factories, system integrators & automation teams
hero.footnote: For plant managers, project engineers and owners who are tired of endless email threads.

1.label: 01 · RFQ intake
1.text: Clean brief with drawings, throughput and standards in one place.

2.label: 02 · Bench & matching
2.text: Curated bench of factories filtered by process, region and envelope.

3.label: 03 · Decision layer
3.text: Comparable quotes, duty impact and risk points in a single view.

orbit.footerValue: 5–12 business days from RFQ to award

home.metricsNote: NovaNexus connects production-ready buyers and vetted factories through a single qualified bench – USA, Europe, Korea and a wider APAC footprint.

pillars.title: A sourcing cockpit for both sides of the table.
pillars.sub: Buyers get clarity. Suppliers get signal. Everyone sees the same version of the project – instead of fifteen different email threads.

buyer.title: For buyers & plant teams
buyer.body: Operations, engineering and project teams that run real lines – not test rigs.
bullets: Structured RFQs so suppliers see the real requirement from day one.
More realistic expectations on budget, lead time and duty before you commit.
A bench of factories you can reuse for follow-on projects.

supplier.title: For suppliers & integrators
supplier.body: Factories in Korea & APAC, system integrators and automation teams that actually build, install and support lines.
bullets: RFQs that fit your machines, certifications and installation envelope.
Less time cleaning up chaotic drawings, more time actually quoting.
Signal on what global buyers will ask for next.

both.title: For both sides – long term
both.body: A shared surface instead of buried emails and scattered spreadsheets.
bullets: Project history you can plug straight into the next RFQ.
Shared context for operations, finance and leadership.
Clearer decisions on where you should really build your next line.

flow.title: From RFQ upload to awarded PO in three clear steps.
flow.sub: The same structure for every project – whether you’re specifying a single saw or a full shuttle-rack system.

1.title: Upload a structured RFQ
1.body: Drawings, throughput, standards and target dates in a clean intake instead of scattered attachments.

2.title: Match to the right factories
2.body: Curated bench by process, region, envelope and certifications – no mass blast to random vendors.

3.title: Clarify, compare, award
3.body: Clarifications, quotes and award live in one thread so operations, engineering and finance stay in sync.

capabilities.title: Lines, processes and regions we actually cover.
capabilities.sub: A deliberately curated bench instead of a generic directory. Every factory and SI is mapped by process, certification, envelope and installation capability.
capabilities.note: The bench is kept intentionally lean. New suppliers are added only after running real projects together.

production.label: Production lines
production.detail: Aluminum extrusion, racking & AS/RS, press & coil lines, conveyors, packaging and end-of-line.

precision.label: Precision & assemblies
precision.detail: Machined parts, fixtures & tooling, cleanroom equipment, medtech and EV components.

regions.label: Regions
regions.detail: Korea-led bench with coverage for the US, EU and Southeast Asia – including build and installation.

final.title: Ready to send a real RFQ – instead of another ‘quick question’ email?
final.sub: Start with a production-ready project. We help translate drawings, throughput and constraints into a clean RFQ and route it to the right factories.

hero.body: NovaNexus sits as a neutral layer between buyers and suppliers in precision manufacturing and industrial automation. The goal is simple:
hero.bodyStrong: fewer surprises before anyone signs a PO.

map.bodyPrefix: Processes, capacity and risk translated into
map.bodyStrong: a shared map both sides can work from.

pillars.heading: What actually sits behind the listings.
pillars.subheading: Most industrial directories maximize the number of logos. NovaNexus is deliberately focused – on depth of capability, real capacity and the work it takes to run a line, not just contact fields.

capability.label: Capability map
capability.title: Processes, not just company logos.
capability.body: Every supplier is anchored by processes, materials, tolerances and certifications – so buyers can search the way engineers think.
bullets: Process tags across machining, extrusion, automation, cleanroom and more.
Material coverage and typical tolerances visible at a glance.
Certifications and industry focus clearly highlighted.

rfqs.label: Structured RFQs
rfqs.title: RFQs that can be quoted cleanly the first time.
rfqs.body: Guided RFQ intake removes guesswork so suppliers respond with real numbers, not “call us to discuss”.
bullets: Required fields for drawings, volumes and quality requirements.
Upload drawings, BOMs and reference photos.
Automatic nudges when a critical detail is missing.

decision.label: Decision layer
decision.title: Playbooks above the listings.
decision.body: Lightweight guides sit right next to suppliers so teams can decide faster and with less politics.
bullets: Make-or-buy and route comparisons for common cases.
Checklists for first-time sourcing and new regions.
Signals on where capacity and lead times are shifting.

buyers.title: If you sit on the buyer side
buyers.copy: Built for operations, engineering and sourcing teams that need a shared, realistic map before a PO goes out.
benefits: A shortlist of realistic options by process, region and budget band.
Cleaner RFQs that bring back real quotes quickly.
Early visibility into risk, lead times and rough cost bands.

suppliers.title: If you sit on the supplier side
suppliers.copy: Your profile here should feel lighter than a full corporate site, but far more informative than a logo in a directory.
benefits: A way to show depth without hiring a marketing agency.
Context on what global buyers are actually asking for this year.
Signals on where to place your next capacity or automation bets.

roadmap.heading: Where this is headed.
roadmap.copy: The roadmap follows how real CAPEX teams buy and build lines – not a shiny feature list. The more projects run through NovaNexus, the sharper guidance and benchmarking become.

now.title: Curated supplier bench & structured intake
now.body: Curated supplier bench, structured RFQ intake and early playbooks so both sides know what “good” looks like from day one.

next.title: Deeper filters, clearer bands
next.body: Richer discovery filters, broader automation coverage and clearer cost and lead-time bands for common project types.

later.title: Analytics, heatmaps and field guides
later.body: Lightweight analytics for suppliers, regional heatmaps for buyers and deeper “how to scope it” guides based on live project data.

cta.heading: Explore the ecosystem.
cta.copy: Browse suppliers, read the guides and send a test RFQ. Even if you’re just mapping options, you should leave with a clearer, more realistic view of what’s possible for your next line or cell.
cta.overline: Start from the side that fits you best.
cta.note: No fees or commitments for browsing.

suppliers.heroTitle: A clean stream of realistic RFQs, not noise.
suppliers.heroSubtitle: NovaNexus filters out weak leads so factories and system integrators only see projects they can realistically win and deliver.

benefits.fitBody: RFQs are matched by process window, envelope, certifications and installation reach.
benefits.contextBody: You see throughput, duty assumptions and risk flags from the start instead of guessing over email.
benefits.supportBody: Support on scoping, quoting and installation planning so you can stay focused on execution.

auth.notesLabel: What types of projects do you plan or supply?
auth.successMessage: Thanks – we’ve received your details and will follow up shortly.

join.subtitle: A secure login for RFQs, quotes and project status. Join on the buyer side, supplier side, or both – and connect real projects to the right factories.
join.metaRegionValue: Cross-border projects across North America, Europe and Korea · APAC hubs.
join.formIntroTitle: Set up your creator account for NovaNexus.
join.formIntroBody: This login holds RFQs, quotes and project threads. You can invite additional plant, engineering or sourcing teammates later from inside the portal.

placeholders.name: Your full name
placeholders.notes: Examples: EV battery module line in the US, AS/RS warehouse in the EU, extrusion + machining for building profiles in APAC…

join.roleHelper: This only shapes onboarding. You can use NovaNexus on both sides of a project later.
join.primaryAccountNote: This is your primary creator account. You can add additional teammates or integrators later from inside the portal.
join.buyerSideLabel: Strong fits on the buyer side
join.supplierSideLabel: What we look for on the supplier side
buyerSignals: Plant / OEM teams planning real CAPEX projects (lines, cells, warehouses, automation).
RFQs where drawings, throughput and rough budget bands are already defined.
Projects where factories in Korea · APAC are a realistic option – not just an afterthought.
supplierSignals: Factories with a proven export track record or installation partners in target regions.
Shops that own both manufacturing and on-site commissioning – not just machine sales.
Teams that like working from structured RFQs, drawings and target standards.
join.loginControlsBody: RFQs, quotes, project history and central files across your lines and warehouses.
join.accessBody: Each teammate gets their own login. You can revoke or rotate access at any time.
join.successMessage: Account created. You’ll be redirected to your NovaNexus portal in a moment.

errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.generic: We couldn’t create this account. Please check your details and try again.

hero.titleLine2: from first idea to running line.
hero.subtitle: One RFQ in, one clear pipeline out. The goal is simple: fewer email chains, fewer “what are we actually working on?” calls and more lines that start up on time.

01.title: Describe the line or project
01.body: You give a concise brief: process, materials, annual volume, region, target budget and timeline. You can upload layouts, drawings or photos directly.

02.title: RFQ is cleaned up and structured
02.body: Missing details are flagged, scope is sharpened and the RFQ is turned into a structured package that suppliers can quote from immediately.

03.title: Bench & matching
03.body: The project is routed only to suppliers whose capabilities, region and minimum project size truly match the scope – not just to whoever happens to be closest.

04.title: Focused conversations
04.body: All candidates see the same structured brief. Questions revolve around options and trade-offs – not “what exactly are you asking for?”

05.title: Decision support
05.body: You get context on cost drivers, lead-time bands and risk points so you can pick a path that fits your plant – not just the cheapest quote.

stepsSection.title: What actually happens after you click “Create RFQ”.
stepsSection.subtitle: The nuances differ, but every project runs along the same backbone so expectations stay aligned across plants, regions and suppliers.

rolesSection.title: Two sides of the same production line.

buyers.title: For buyers & operations
buyers.point1: Cleaner RFQs and fewer ping-pong emails.
buyers.point2: A shortlist of realistic suppliers per project.
buyers.point3: A better feel for cost and lead-time bands before you commit.

suppliers.title: For factories & integrators
suppliers.point1: Requests that match your processes and minimum project sizes.
suppliers.point2: Structured information from day one instead of vague one-liners.
suppliers.point3: A place to show depth of capability, not just a logo.

buyerSection.title: How NovaNexus fits into a buyer’s week.
buyerSection.subtitle: NovaNexus is not another system you need to roll out across the plant. It’s a structured workspace your team can throw projects into once a clean RFQ and realistic bench of factories are needed.

step1.title: Capture the real requirement once, cleanly.
step1.point1: Upload layouts, drawings, photos and data on the current line.
step1.point2: Tag the RFQ by plant, line, process, safety standard and target go-live.
step1.point3: Add throughput targets, budget bands and duty / FTA assumptions.
step1.point4: Share the workspace with operations, maintenance and finance via a single link.

step2.title: A bench of factories that can actually deliver.
step2.point1: NovaNexus filters suppliers by process capability, certifications and region.
step2.point2: You see a shortlist of 3–7 realistic options instead of a directory with hundreds of entries.
step2.point3: Each candidate shows reference projects, typical lead times and cost bands.
step2.point4: Internal comments stay within your team – suppliers only see what they need.

step3.title: Decide with less noise and more context.
step3.point1: Quotes arrive in the same structure – comparisons take minutes, not days.
step3.point2: You see how duty, freight and local installation change true landed cost.
step3.point3: You capture why a supplier was selected or declined – for audits and repeat projects.
step3.point4: Export a clear summary for CAPEX reviews and management approvals.

supplierSection.title: What “good” looks like on NovaNexus for factories.
supplierSection.subtitle: The best suppliers behave like engineering partners, not quote machines. Here’s how high-performing factories use NovaNexus to protect capacity and win the right projects.

before.title: Sharpen your profile so the right jobs find you.
before.point1: Define your sweet spots: processes, tonnage, materials and minimum ticket size.
before.point2: Upload reference lines with photos, videos and real throughput numbers.
before.point3: Keep certifications, safety standards and countries of origin current.
before.point4: Block capacity windows so buyers only see realistic lead times.

during.title: Qualify fast and quote without chaos.
during.point1: Score each RFQ on fit: process match, volume, timing and strategic value.
during.point2: Ask structured questions on drawings instead of long email chains.
during.point3: Quote with clear breakdown: equipment, options, installation, training.
during.point4: Flag technical risks early so FAT and SAT don’t surface surprises.

after.title: Deliver cleanly and earn the next project.
after.point1: Lock in milestones: design freeze, FAT, shipment, installation, ramp-up.
after.point2: Share on-site requirements, checklists and drawings in the project workspace.
after.point3: Log change orders with reasons so buyer finance and management stay aligned.
after.point4: Capture lessons learned to raise your hit rate on similar RFQs.

summary.signalBody: See which sectors and regions are heating up – from live RFQ volume, not rumor.
summary.focusBody: Spend time on fewer, better-qualified RFQs that fit your lines and margin goals.
summary.partnershipBody: Buyers see that you cover build, installation and long-term support – not just the first shipment.

pipeline.title: From RFQ upload to awarded PO in three clear stages.
pipeline.subtitle: Every project follows the same backbone – whether it’s a single saw or a full shuttle-rack system. That consistency keeps programs on track.

stage1.title: Upload once, auto-structure.
stage1.point1: Upload drawings, BOMs, photos and line data in standard formats.
stage1.point2: Tag safety standards, certifications, region and target start date.
stage1.point3: Auto-generate a clean RFQ summary suppliers can quote from immediately.
stage1.point4: Let internal stakeholders comment before anything goes out to vendors.

stage2.title: Match to the right factories and align scope.
stage2.point1: NovaNexus proposes a curated bench that fits process, scale and region.
stage2.point2: Suppliers confirm feasibility and highlight trade-offs instead of guessing.
stage2.point3: Quotes come back in comparable format with options and lead-time scenarios.
stage2.point4: All clarifications and revisions stay anchored to the project, not scattered in inboxes.

stage3.title: Decide and keep execution visible.
stage3.point1: Select a supplier and capture decision logic for audits and repeat projects.
stage3.point2: Track key milestones against the agreed schedule and responsibilities.
stage3.point3: Store FAT reports, installation photos and SAT sign-offs in the same workspace.
stage3.point4: Use post-project reviews to continuously sharpen your preferred supplier bench.

summary.oneSpineBody: Every RFQ follows the same structure – from pilot lines to full plant expansions.
summary.contextBody: Engineering, operations, finance and leadership all see the same live picture.
summary.knowledgeBody: Completed projects become templates instead of disappearing into email archives.

finalCta.title: Start with a real project.
finalCta.subtitle: Submit an RFQ, browse the supplier bench or use the content to sharpen your options. You should leave with a clearer view than when you arrived.

hero.title: Live RFQs across lines, robotics and industrial automation.
hero.subtitle: A rotating, anonymized snapshot of RFQs running through NovaNexus – CNC and press lines, warehouse systems, EV cells plus medical and cleanroom projects where engineering quality matters more than the lowest bid.
hero.note: Representative sample projects by region. Live RFQs appear here automatically while active.

americas.label: US & Americas
americas.headline: North-America-led, with additional capacity in LATAM.

europe.label: Europe
europe.headline: Precision-heavy projects across DACH, Nordics and beyond.

apac.label: Asia-Pacific
apac.headline: Korea-led bench with selected partners across wider APAC.

filters.note: Filters only change the view. Underneath, every row is a fully scoped RFQ with drawings, throughput and standards.

table.empty: No RFQs match the current filters.

sampleRows.title: Cleanroom assembly cells for medical devices

final.title: Should your next RFQ live here instead of an email chain?
final.body: Start with a production-ready project. We help translate drawings, throughput and constraints into a clean RFQ and route it to a realistic bench of factories.

hero.title: Start with a real project.
hero.subtitle: This form is for lines, cells, retrofits and automation where choosing the wrong supplier really hurts. Be concrete: what runs today, what has to change and where the line will live later.
hero.noteBody: RFQs are reviewed, cleaned up and matched to a curated bench of factories and integrators. There is no “send to all suppliers” button – every project is routed deliberately.
hero.noteFoot: If something is sensitive, you can keep names generic here and share drawings later under NDA.

basics.title: Project basics
basics.subtitle: Just enough context that a technical sales or engineering lead understands what type of line or cell this is.

scope.title: Scope & location
scope.subtitle: Region and industry help narrow the bench. If you’re flexible, mention it under constraints.

tech.title: Technical description
tech.subtitle: Bullets are ideal. Focus on must-haves – layout, interfaces with existing equipment, safety, cleanroom, local service, certifications.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Used only to come back to this RFQ. In the MVP there’s no public profile tied to this form.

company.label: Company / organization (optional)
company.placeholder: Example: Tier-1 automotive supplier, 3PL logistics, medtech OEM…

projectName.label: Project name *
projectName.placeholder: Example: new aluminum extrusion line for building profiles

process.label: Core process / line focus *
process.placeholder: Example: extrusion & hot saws, shuttle racking, palletizing cells…

annualVolume.label: Annual volume / throughput (rough)
annualVolume.placeholder: Example: 15,000 t / year, 500 pallets / day, 40 modules / hour…

description.label: What do you want to build or upgrade? *
description.placeholder:
- New extrusion line to replace aging equipment
- Shuttle racks integrated into existing WMS
- Robot palletizer cell added after current case packer…

constraints.label: Key constraints / must-haves
constraints.placeholder:
- Existing building layout / ceiling height
- Local service required in a specific country
- Cleanroom / validation / certification requirements…

validation.process: Core process / line focus is required.
validation.description: Please describe what you want to build or upgrade.

errors.noBackend: The RFQ backend is not reachable from this environment. Please check PocketBase URL and client configuration.
errors.saveFailed: There was an error saving this RFQ. Please check the RFQ collection, API rules and PocketBase URL.

success.title: Your RFQ has been created.
success.body: The RFQ is now in the review queue. You’ll hear from us once it’s structured and matched to a realistic bench of factories.

hero.title: Start with a project or a factory.
hero.body: Give a short sketch of what you’re working on. We’ll come back with a clear next step – from RFQ intake to a supplier intro or a quick reality check on scope and budget.

buyers.title: For buyers
buyers.body: Planning a new line, retrofit or warehouse project and want to see whether NovaNexus fits?
bullets: ▢ Short project overview (product, volume, timing).
▢ Region / preferred installation window.
▢ Constraints we should know from day one.

suppliers.title: For suppliers
suppliers.body: Factory, integrator or automation shop that wants to be considered for matched projects?
bullets: ▢ Core processes and equipment you’re strong in.
▢ Typical project sizes / ticket range.
▢ Certifications and reference installations.

email.title: Direct email
email.body: Prefer to write directly? Send a short note with your role and what you’re trying to solve. You can attach layouts, photos or existing RFQs if helpful.
email.footnote: For active projects we typically respond within 1–2 business days.
